message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Building the 점점 (Dotton) iOS diary app from scratch. The app fills calendar day-circles as users record daily entries, forming a pixel-art image each month. The user wants to develop screen-by-screen, starting with tech stack confirmation, then Phase 1 Foundation, then Phase 2-1 (calendar grid). The user explicitly requested: no tab bar, Firebase deferred to last phase, screen development one at a time with direct instruction per phase. The user has just provided complete Phase 2-1 UI specs and the assistant started implementing (only one file changed before summary was triggered).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Swift / SwiftUI** — iOS platform
   - **TCA (The Composable Architecture) 1.17+** — `@Reducer` macro, `@ObservableState`, `@DependencyClient`
   - **SwiftData** — `@Model` for `Diary`, ModelContainer in DottonApp
   - **PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup** — Xcode 26 feature; all files in `Dotton/` folder auto-included in build target (no manual pbxproj file registration needed)
   - **`@unchecked Sendable`** — Required on `Diary` (`@Model` class) to satisfy TCA's `@Sendable` closure requirements
   - **Sunday-start calendar grid** — weekday header "일월화수목금토"; `firstWeekdayOffset = weekday - 1`
   - **Monday-start → Sunday-start** — Changed in `Date+Helpers.swift`
   - **PostScript font names** — Used in `Font.custom()`, differ from filenames
   - **Info.plist at project root** — Moved outside `Dotton/` to avoid "Multiple commands produce" conflict with file system sync group
   - **`GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE = NO`** + `INFOPLIST_FILE = Info.plist` — Set in pbxproj
   - **Swift Charts** — For statistics (Phase 5, no 3rd party needed)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/CLAUDE.md`**
     - Project spec: app overview, tech stack, screen structure

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/DottonApp.swift`**
     - Currently simplified (no TCA store yet — removed after circular reference error)
     - Needs update in Phase 2-1 to use AppFeature store
     ```swift
     import SwiftUI
     import SwiftData
     @main
     struct DottonApp: App {
         var body: some Scene {
             WindowGroup { AppView() }
             .modelContainer(for: Diary.self)
         }
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Models/Diary.swift`**
     - SwiftData `@Model`, `@unchecked Sendable` for TCA compatibility
     ```swift
     @Model
     final class Diary: @unchecked Sendable {
         var id: UUID
         var date: Date
         var achievementAnswer: String
         var gratitudeAnswer: String
         var freeAnswer: String
         var keywords: [String]  // DiscoveryKeyword raw values
         var createdAt: Date
         var discoveryKeywords: [DiscoveryKeyword] { keywords.compactMap { DiscoveryKeyword(rawValue: $0) } }
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Models/DiscoveryKeyword.swift`**
     - 15 keywords across 3 sections (성장/성취, 연결, 자기돌봄)
     - Note: user changed `DiscoverySection.growth` rawValue from "성장/성취" to "성취"

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Models/MonthlyTheme.swift`**
     ```swift
     struct MonthlyTheme {
         var year: Int
         var month: Int
         var sentence: String
         var emoji: String
         var pictureTitle: String   // display text shown at completion (NOT asset name)
         var primaryColor: Color
         var secondaryColor: Color
         var pixelArtCells: [PixelArtCell]
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Models/PixelArtCell.swift`**
     ```swift
     struct PixelArtCell: Equatable {
         var gridIndex: Int        // 0-based position in 7×N grid
         var backgroundColor: Color
         var textColor: Color
         var text: String?
         var imageName: String?    // Xcode asset name for image cells
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Clients/DiaryClient.swift`**
     - TCA `@DependencyClient`; all methods are stub (Phase 3 implementation pending)
     ```swift
     @DependencyClient
     struct DiaryClient: Sendable {
         var fetchDiaries: @Sendable (_ month: Date) async throws -> [Diary] = { _ in [] }
         var fetchDiary: @Sendable (_ date: Date) async throws -> Diary? = { _ in nil }
         var saveDiary: @Sendable (_ diary: Diary) async throws -> Void
         var deleteDiary: @Sendable (_ diary: Diary) async throws -> Void
     }
     extension DependencyValues {
         var diaryClient: DiaryClient { get { self[DiaryClient.self] } set { ... } }
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Extensions/Date+Helpers.swift`**
     - **Just updated**: changed `firstWeekdayOffset` to Sunday-start
     ```swift
     var firstWeekdayOffset: Int {
         let weekday = Calendar.current.component(.weekday, from: startOfMonth)
         // 1=일(Sun)..7=토(Sat) → Sun=0, Mon=1, ..., Sat=6
         return weekday - 1
     }
     var startOfMonth: Date { ... }
     var endOfMonth: Date { ... }
     var daysInMonth: Int { ... }
     var calendarGridCount: Int { ... }  // rounds up to multiple of 7
     var monthYear: String { ... }
     func isSameDay(as other: Date) -> Bool { ... }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Extensions/Color+Hex.swift`**
     ```swift
     extension Color {
         init(hex: String) { /* supports 6 and 8 char hex */ }
     }
     ```

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Core/Data/MonthlyThemeData.swift`**
     - 12 months hardcoded: 2026 Apr–Dec, 2027 Jan–Mar
     - Helper functions `cell()` and `imageCell()` for building PixelArtCells
     - `MonthlyTheme.all` array ordered chronologically (Apr 2026 → Mar 2027)
     - Each theme has: sentence, emoji, pictureTitle, primaryColor, secondaryColor, pixelArtCells
     - May(5월) and August(8월) have 42 cells (6 rows); others have 35 cells (5 rows)
     - Note: 3월 data was modified by user (changed some colors, e.g. AF9989→F2E5D0, fixed cell 28 text)

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Utils/Fonts/Font+Custom.swift`**
     ```swift
     extension Font {
         static func gulim(_ size: CGFloat) -> Font { .custom("TTGulimB", size: size) }
         static func meetme(_ size: CGFloat) -> Font { .custom("Ownglyph_meetme-Rg", size: size) }
         static func yeongdeokSea(_ size: CGFloat) -> Font { .custom("Yeongdeok Sea", size: size) }
     }
     ```
     Font files renamed: `210Gulim.ttf`, `meetme.ttf`, `yeongdeokSea.ttf` (in `Utils/Fonts/`)

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Info.plist`** (at project root, NOT inside Dotton/)
     - Contains `UIAppFonts` array with 3 font filenames
     - `GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE = NO`, `INFOPLIST_FILE = Info.plist` in pbxproj

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Features/AppFeature/AppFeature.swift`**
     - Currently just a comment placeholder (Phase 2 will fill this)

   - **`/Users/user/Desktop/Dotton/Dotton/Features/AppFeature/AppView.swift`**
     - Currently placeholder showing "점점" text, no TCA store

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`@Reducer` circular reference** — `AppFeature` with empty `Action` enum caused macro expansion circular reference. Fixed by removing all TCA code from AppFeature (just a comment) and making AppView/DottonApp TCA-free for Phase 1.
   - **`Diary` not Sendable** — TCA `@DependencyClient` closures require `Sendable` parameters. Fixed by adding `@unchecked Sendable` to `Diary` class (safe because `@Model` is MainActor-bound).
   - **`import Dependencies` fails** — Only `ComposableArchitecture` product is added; `Dependencies` isn't separately importable. Removed the import.
   - **Multiple commands produce Info.plist** — `PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup` auto-includes `Info.plist` as resource AND the build system processes it as Info.plist. Fixed by moving Info.plist outside `Dotton/` to project root.
   - **MissedDayPreview.swift errors** — Created a preview file that had `Color(hex:)` label issue and `UIColor` not in scope. User asked to delete it; file was removed.
   - **replace_all replaced wrong occurrences** — When renaming `imageName` to `pictureTitle` in MonthlyThemeData.swift, it also replaced `imageName` inside the `imageCell()` helper function. Fixed by reverting those specific lines.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Confirmed `PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup` means no manual file registration needed in pbxproj — all files in `Dotton/` folder are auto-compiled.
   - SwiftData + TCA coexistence: Using stub DiaryClient for Phase 1-2, real implementation in Phase 3. `Diary: @unchecked Sendable` solves the Sendable boundary issue.
   - Font PostScript names extracted via Python script from TTF binary name tables.
   - Decided against JSON for hardcoded theme data (Color type not serializable; data never changes at runtime).

6. All user messages:
   - "CLAUDE.md에다가 [앱 개요]... 이 내용 좀 넣어줄래?"
   - "/plan" — requested plan mode
   - "이 앱을 만들때 어떤식으로 작업할지 계획 세워봐. 화면 하나씩 개발했으면 좋겠고, 우선 처음에는 기술 스택부터 확정하고 갔으면 좋겠어"
   - "좋아. 근데 내가 plan을 수행하면 너가 모든 단계를 한번에 구현하는 구조야?"
   - (selected "직접 지시" for execution style)
   - "음 응 근데 Tab을 따로 두는 구조는 아니라서. 탭은 안 둬도 될 것 같고, firebase는 필수 기능은 아니라 맨 마지막 phase로 따로 빼줘."
   - "통계 화면이랑 공유 기능은 메인 화면 상단 우측에 버튼이 있어. 그리고 일기 작성은 원 탭으로 진입하는거 맞아"
   - "Phase1 실행해줘"
   - "추가 완." (TCA SPM added)
   - "지금 빌드 실패나는거 고쳐봐."
   - "DiaryEntry -> 이름 이상함.. entry ?? 무슨 뜻인지 잘 안 와닿음 / answer1,2,3 이렇게 하면.. 나중에 질문 순서 바뀌면 애매해짐 / PixelArtCell에서 dayNumber가 애매한게…"
   - "ㅇㅇ Diary로 깔끔하게 갈게"
   - "음 응 근데 Tab을 따로..." (repeated navigation clarification)
   - [Provided full 12-month pixel art data]
   - "아맞다. 이게 년도마다도 그림이 달라질거라... 4월~12월은 2026년, 1~3월은 2027년으로 해줄 수 있어?"
   - "ㅇㅇ 근데 pictureKeyword랑 sentence랑 겹치는데 하나만 유지해도 될것 같고, imageName은 내가 불러줄게. 이모지도 하나씩 할당할라하는데 그것도 내가 불러주면 될까"
   - [Provided emoji and pictureTitle for all 12 months]
   - "아 근데 imageName이라는 작명이 좀 헷갈리나? 저걸로 이미지를 찾는게 아니고, 저건 그림 완성되었을 때 유저한테 보여주는 텍스트야"
   - "굿. phase2 하기 전에 내가 전달해줘야 하는 정보 있어?"
   - "phase 2를 두단계로 나누자. phase2-1, 2-2 이렇게. 달력 부분이랑 아래 하단 노트를 각각 나누자"
   - "잠깐만. 그 전에.. 지금 이게 달력 셀 ui인데, 기록을 안하고 지나간 날에 대해서 어떤 식으로 표현하면 좋을까?" [with screenshots]

   - "너가 추가한 파일 삭제해" (re: MissedDayPreview.swift)
   - "깨진 느낌 UI는? 세개다 줘봐. localhost로 html 파일 주는건 안돼? 피그마에 넣어서 보려고"

   - "이제 phase2-1 질문 답할까? 질문이 뭐였지?" (repeated)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Phase 2-1 (In Progress)**: Implement calendar grid UI
     - `UI/Components/DotCircle.swift` — 5 circle states (recorded, future, missed, today overlay, preInstall)
     - `UI/Components/CalendarGrid.swift` — 7×5 or 7×6 grid + weekday header
     - `Features/Main/MainFeature.swift` — TCA reducer with month navigation, install date logic
     - `Features/Main/MainView.swift` — Full main screen layout
     - Update `Features/AppFeature/AppFeature.swift` — Wrap MainFeature
     - Update `Features/AppFeature/AppView.swift` — Scope to MainView

   Implementing Phase 2-1 (calendar grid). The user provided detailed specs. The only file changed so far was updating `Date+Helpers.swift` to use Sunday-start `firstWeekdayOffset`:

   ```swift
   // Changed from Monday-start to Sunday-start
   var firstWeekdayOffset: Int {
       let weekday = Calendar.current.component(.weekday, from: startOfMonth)

       return weekday - 1

   ```

   The following Phase 2-1 specs were provided (not yet implemented):
   - Circle size: 50×50, horizontal: no spacing, vertical: 16pt margin
   - **5 circle states**: recorded (MonthlyTheme color+text, meetme font 24), future (EDECE9 bg, date text BBBBBB, 210Gulim 18), missed (theme bg 40% opacity + dashed inner stroke theme color 70% + date text, 210Gulim 18), today (additional F58583 inner stroke 2.5pt), preInstall (same as recorded)
   - Weekday header: "일월화수목금토", 210Gulim 13, 일=C96458, 토=5476BA, 평일=A2A19F, 13pt below header text to grid
   - Month header: "N월" format, 210Gulim 20, black; icon_arrow_left/right (8×4); 20pt gap between text and arrows; year above month row 8pt, leading aligned with left arrow
   - Navigation: can't go before install month, can go +1 year forward
   - Top buttons: icon_statistics + icon_export, 20×20 icons in 38×38 white circle with shadow(0,1,4,8%)
   - Background: FAF8F5, top padding: 80pt

9. Optional Next Step:
   Continue Phase 2-1 implementation. Per the user's latest spec message, the next files to create/update are:
   - `UI/Components/DotCircle.swift` (DotState enum + DotCircle view)
   - `UI/Components/CalendarGrid.swift` (7×N grid + weekday header)
   - `Features/Main/MainFeature.swift` (TCA reducer)
   - `Features/Main/MainView.swift` (full layout per specs)
   - Update `AppFeature.swift`, `AppView.swift`, `DottonApp.swift` to wire TCA back in

   The user specified: "원의 크기: 50x50 / 가로: 마진 없이 딱 붙여서 / 세로: 16마진" and all circle states, weekday/month header specs, background color FAF8F5, and button layout. The `Date+Helpers.swift` Sunday-start change was the first step; the remaining implementation has not yet begun.

import SwiftUI

// MARK: - Cell 생성 헬퍼 (0-based gridIndex 자동 변환)

private func cell(
    _ index: Int,
    bg: String,
    text: String? = nil,
    textColor: String? = nil
) -> PixelArtCell {
    PixelArtCell(
        gridIndex: index - 1,
        backgroundColor: Color(hex: bg),
        textColor: textColor.map { Color(hex: $0) } ?? .clear,
        text: text,
        imageName: nil
    )
}

private func imageCell(_ index: Int, name: String) -> PixelArtCell {
    PixelArtCell(
        gridIndex: index - 1,
        backgroundColor: .clear,
        textColor: .clear,
        text: nil,
        imageName: name
    )
}

// MARK: - MonthlyTheme 데이터

extension MonthlyTheme {
    // 2026년 4월 ~ 2027년 3월
    static let all: [MonthlyTheme] = [
        april, may, june, july, august, september, october, november, december,
        january, february, march
    ]

    // MARK: 1월 (2027) - 새로움의 시작
    static let january = MonthlyTheme(
        year: 2027,
        month: 1,
        sentence: "새로움의 시작",
        emoji: "🌅",
        pictureTitle: "떠오르는 해",
        primaryColor: Color(hex: "F8C787"),
        secondaryColor: Color(hex: "3C6583"),
        pixelArtCells: [
            cell(1,  bg: "F8C787", text: "새", textColor: "F0974A"),

**Key decisions:**
- Today + unrecorded → shows as `future` state + today ring (user can still record)

- Padding cells = outside current month days → show pixel art color, no date

1→import ComposableArchitecture
     2→import Foundation
     3→
     4→@Reducer
     5→struct MainFeature {
     6→    @ObservableState
     7→    struct State: Equatable {
     8→        var currentMonth: Date = Calendar.current.startOfDay(for: Date()).startOfMonth
     9→        var diaries: [Diary] = []
    10→        var installDate: Date = Date()
    11→    }
    12→
    13→    enum Action {
    14→        case onAppear
    15→        case prevMonthTapped
    16→        case nextMonthTapped
    17→        case dayTapped(Date)
    18→        case diariesLoaded([Diary])
    19→        case statisticsTapped
    20→        case exportTapped
    21→    }
    22→
    23→    @Dependency(\.diaryClient) var diaryClient
    24→
    25→    var body: some ReducerOf<Self> {
    26→        Reduce { state, action in
    27→            switch action {
    28→            case .onAppear:
    29→                if let saved = UserDefaults.standard.object(forKey: "installDate") as? Date {
    30→                    state.installDate = saved
    31→                } else {
    32→                    let now = Date()
    33→                    UserDefaults.standard.set(now, forKey: "installDate")
    34→                    state.installDate = now
    35→                }
    36→                return loadDiaries(for: state.currentMonth)
    37→
    38→            case .prevMonthTapped:
    39→                let prev = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .month, value: -1, to: state.currentMonth)!
    40→                guard prev >= state.installDate.startOfMonth else { return .none }
    41→                state.currentMonth = prev
    42→                return loadDiaries(for: state.currentMonth)
    43→
    44→            case .nextMonthTapped:
    45→                let next = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .month, value: 1, to: state.currentMonth)!
    46→                let maxMonth = Calendar.current.date(byAdding: .month, value: 12, to: Date())!.startOfMonth
    47→                guard next <= maxMonth else { return .none }
    48→                state.currentMonth = next
    49→                return loadDiaries(for: state.currentMonth)
    50→
    51→            case .dayTapped:

import Foundation
import SwiftData

@Model
final class Diary: @unchecked Sendable {
    var id: UUID
    var date: Date
    var achievementAnswer: String
    var gratitudeAnswer: String
    var freeAnswer: String
    var keywords: [String]
    var createdAt: Date

    init(
        id: UUID = UUID(),
        date: Date,
        achievementAnswer: String = "",
        gratitudeAnswer: String = "",
        freeAnswer: String = "",
        keywords: [String] = [],
        createdAt: Date = Date()
    ) {
        self.id = id
        self.date = date
        self.achievementAnswer = achievementAnswer
        self.gratitudeAnswer = gratitudeAnswer
        self.freeAnswer = freeAnswer
        self.keywords = keywords
        self.createdAt = createdAt
    }

    var discoveryKeywords: [DiscoveryKeyword] {
        keywords.compactMap { DiscoveryKeyword(rawValue: $0) }
    }
}

## About TCA (The Composable Architecture)

The Composable Architecture (TCA, for short) is a library for building applications in a consistent and understandable way, with composition, testing, and ergonomics in mind. It can be used in SwiftUI, UIKit, and more, and on any Apple platform (iOS, macOS, iPadOS, visionOS, tvOS, and watchOS).

The Composable Architecture is a Swift adaptation of the Redux framework, that matches perfectly with SwiftUI, UIKit, Combine, and even Modern Concurrency (async/await).

## Key Features

TCA provides state management capabilities and composition tools to break down large features into smaller components that can be extracted to their own, isolated modules and be easily glued back together to form the feature.

    14→    struct State: Equatable {

    18→

    20→        case onLoad
    21→        case triggerAnimation
    22→        case animationStopped

    25→    @Dependency(\.continuousClock) private var clock
    26→    private let animationDuration = 0.8
    27→    private let waitingDuration = 1.0
    28→
    29→    var body: some ReducerOf<Self> {
    30→        Reduce<State, Action> { state, action in
    31→            switch action {
    32→            case .onLoad:
    33→                return .run { send in
    34→                    try await clock.sleep(for: .seconds(waitingDuration))
    35→                    await send(.triggerAnimation, animation: .linear(duration: animationDuration))
    36→                }
    37→
    38→            case .triggerAnimation:
    39→                state.isAnimating = true
    40→
    41→                return .run { send in
    42→                    try await clock.sleep(for: .seconds(animationDuration + 0.8))
    43→                    await send(.animationStopped)
    44→                }
    45→
    46→            case .animationStopped:
    47→                state.isFinished = true

# Version 1.25.2 Key Changes

Based on the release notes, version 1.25.2 addresses two main areas:

**Bug Fixes in Enum Scope Feature:**
The release fixed "a few issues in the newly introduced enum scope feature" including a binding dismissal bug and a regression affecting compilation performance for scope operations (PR #3887).

**Deprecation Handling:**
Trait-based deprecations now receive "soft-deprecated" status when the associated trait isn't enabled (PR #3885), allowing for more graceful handling of deprecated features.

The release notes don't specifically mention circular references or the @Reducer macro directly, though the enum scope feature improvements may impact how these constructs interact with the framework.

# Swift Composable Architecture 1.25.0 Changes

Based on the release notes, version 1.25.0 introduces significant updates alongside deprecations preparing for version 2.0:

## Major New Features

- **Streamlined `onChange` operator** for cleaner state observation
- **Enum scoping** capabilities for better type safety
- **Reentrant action detection** with warnings emitted from the store

## Key Deprecations

The release includes substantial trait and full deprecations:

- `Effect` is being trait-deprecated in favor of `EffectOf`
- `Reducer.reduce` is fully deprecated
- `Store.withState` and binding-related classes (`BindingViewState`/`BindingViewStore`) are deprecated
- Enum state `Scope` and animation-scheduling effects face deprecation

## Important Note

The release includes "a significant batch of deprecations" preparing for Composable Architecture 2.0. The documentation specifically directs users to **consult the migration guide** for complete upgrade details and implementation strategies.

No specific issues are mentioned in the release notes—only infrastructure improvements like updating API endpoints and documentation additions.

# Circular Reference Issues in Swift Composable Architecture

Based on the GitHub issues search, there are several reported problems related to circular references in the TCA `@Reducer` macro:

## Key Issues Found

**Most Recent:** Issue #3808 "Reducer Circular reference" (closed Oct 29, 2025) - the newest report of this problem.

**Notable Pattern:** Multiple issues involve the `@Reducer` macro causing compilation errors when used with extensions:

- Issue #2589: "Circular reference error using @Reducer with an extension on reducer's State" (marked as an "apple bug")
- Issue #2587: "Extension for Reducer.State/Action causes a compilation error with the new @Reducer macros" (also marked "apple bug")

**Related Issue:** Issue #3775 "SourceKit infinite indexing loop with TCA on Xcode 26" involved an indexing loop that may be related to similar structural problems.

## Summary

All reported circular reference issues have been closed as "completed," suggesting they were either resolved in updates or identified as limitations stemming from Apple's Swift compiler implementation rather than TCA itself. The pattern indicates the problem specifically emerges when combining the `@Reducer` macro with state/action extensions.

# Circular Reference Issue with @Reducer and MainActor

Based on the discussion thread, when enabling default main actor isolation in Xcode 26-beta, users encounter a "Circular reference" error when using the `@Reducer` macro.

## The Problem

One developer reported that enabling default main actor isolation causes the compiler to flag a circular reference error pointing to the macro expansion's protocol conformance line. Another user found that `ReducerOf<Self>` produces a build error stating the type "does not conform to protocol 'Reducer'" under the same conditions.

## Current Recommendation

The maintainer acknowledged these are bugs in Swift 6.2's default main actor isolation implementation affecting third-party libraries generally. The suggested workarounds are:

1. **Use explicit syntax**: Replace `ReducerOf<Self>` with `Reducer<State, Action>`
2. **Downgrade temporarily**: Switch to Swift 5 mode if exploring this feature with TCA

The team expects these compiler issues to be resolved in future Swift updates before full support is implemented.

# Circular Reference Error with @Reducer and State Extensions

## The Problem

When migrating to v1.4 of Swift Composable Architecture, using the `@Reducer` macro with extensions on the reducer's `State` type causes compiler circular reference errors. As the issue reporter demonstrated, simply adding an empty extension to `CounterFeature.State` triggers this compilation failure.

## Root Cause

This is not a library bug—it's a Swift compiler limitation. The `@Reducer` macro's implementation creates circular dependencies when the compiler attempts to resolve types within extensions of nested State types, particularly during macro expansion.

## Official Solution

The library maintainers documented this problem and recommend:

1. **Review the documentation**: The Composable Architecture team has documented this issue in their official guides with workarounds.

2. **Report to Apple**: An issue has been filed with Apple's Swift team (referenced as issue #66450). Users experiencing this problem are encouraged to comment on Apple's issue tracker to emphasize how common this pattern is in real applications.

3. **Accept it's compiler-level**: Only Apple can resolve this through Swift compiler improvements, as it falls outside the library's control.

The issue was ultimately closed as expected behavior given current Swift compiler constraints, though it remains a practical limitation for developers using modern Composable Architecture patterns.

# TCA Circular Reference Issue

Based on the gist content, this demonstrates a **circular dependency problem in The Composable Architecture (TCA)** where screens can navigate to each other recursively.

## The Problem

The code shows three screens with circular navigation patterns:
- **Screen A** can navigate to itself or to Screen B
- **Screen B** can navigate back to Screen A
- This creates a circular reference that would normally cause compiler issues

## Solutions Provided

1. **Indirect Enums**: The `ScreenAAction` and `ScreenBAction` enums use the `indirect` keyword, allowing recursive action types that reference themselves or other actions.

2. **Recursive Reducer Pattern**: The code implements a custom `.recurse()` extension that uses a closure to allow the reducer to reference itself, enabling self-contained recursive logic handling.

3. **Cow Property Wrapper**: A custom copy-on-write wrapper manages state mutations efficiently, preventing unnecessary copies while maintaining value semantics required by TCA.

These techniques allow complex navigation hierarchies while maintaining TCA's state management principles without breaking the type system.

## Key Findings on TCA @Reducer Circular Reference Issues

Based on my research, here are the main causes and solutions for circular reference issues in TCA 1.24.x and 1.25.x:

### **Primary Causes:**

1. **Extensions on Reducer State/Action Types**
   - When you add extensions to `Reducer.State` or `Reducer.Action` types, the @Reducer macro's expansion creates circular dependencies during type resolution
   - This is a Swift compiler limitation, not a TCA bug (Apple issue #66450)
   - The compiler struggles to resolve types during macro expansion when nested types are extended

2. **Swift 6 + Default Actor Isolation (Xcode 16)**
   - Enabling "Default Actor Isolation = MainActor" causes the compiler to flag the macro expansion as a circular reference
   - This occurs specifically with `ReducerOf<Self>` syntax
   - The issue is in Swift 6.2's implementation of default main actor isolation, not TCA itself

3. **Macro Compilation in Modularized Projects**
   - When multiple feature modules depend on TCA with macro usage, circular dependency issues can arise during compilation

### **Solutions:**

1. **For State/Action Extensions:**
   - Avoid adding extensions to nested State/Action types if possible
   - Refactor to compute properties within the main reducer struct instead
   - File feedback with Apple (issue #66450) to prioritize compiler fixes

2. **For Swift 6 MainActor Isolation:**
   - Replace `ReducerOf<Self>` with explicit `Reducer<State, Action>` syntax
   - Temporarily downgrade to Swift 5 mode
   - Add `nonisolated` modifiers to reducer structs to bypass isolation checks
   - Mark all reducers as `Sendable`

3. **For Modularized Projects:**
   - Check the macro compilation issue discussion (#3292) on the Swift Composable Architecture repo
   - Consider restructuring module dependencies to avoid circular imports

### **Version Notes:**
- Version 1.25.2 includes enum scope feature improvements but doesn't directly address circular references
- Version 1.25.0 introduced significant deprecations preparing for TCA 2.0—consult the migration guide for full details

Sources:
- [Circular reference error using @Reducer with an extension on reducer's State · Issue #2589](https://github.com/pointfreeco/swift-composable-architecture/issues/2589)

4. 근데 지금 그 conformance를 생성하는 중 → **순환 참조**

`some Reducer<State, Action>` 으로 바꾸면 `Self` 참조가 없으니 순환이 생기지 않아.

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Building the 점점 (Dotton) iOS diary app. The immediate session focused on:
   - Phase 2-1: Implementing the calendar grid UI (DotCircle, CalendarGrid, MainFeature, MainView)
   - Setting up test preview with install date April 2026, current date March 30, 2027, with ~80% diary coverage
   - Fixing a persistent `@Reducer` macro circular reference error
   - Fixing a simulator launch error (missing CFBundleExecutable)
   - 5 UI fixes requested by user: (1) larger arrow touch area, (2) month header left-aligned with grid, (3) year without comma separator, (4) show mock diary data, (5) top button cornerRadius 13

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **TCA (The Composable Architecture) 1.25.2** — `@Reducer` macro, `@ObservableState`, `@DependencyClient`, `some Reducer<State, Action>` (NOT `some ReducerOf<Self>`)
   - **Xcode 26 / Swift 6.2 circular reference bug** — `Default Actor Isolation = MainActor` causes `@Reducer` macro to fail when `body` returns `some ReducerOf<Self>` due to `Self` type resolution cycle. Fix: use `some Reducer<State, Action>` explicitly
   - **SwiftData `@Model` in `@ObservableState`** — Having `[Diary]` (`@Model` class) in TCA state causes macro conflicts; replaced with `[Date]` (recorded dates only)
   - **TCA version mismatch** — Was on 1.17.0; ggaebiz reference project uses 1.23.1; updated to 1.25.2
   - **`PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup`** — Xcode 26 auto-includes all files; no manual pbxproj registration
   - **`CFBundleExecutable` missing** — Info.plist at project root was missing this key; fixed by adding `$(EXECUTABLE_NAME)`
   - **AppFeature eliminated** — Removed `@Reducer struct AppFeature` as wrapper; `MainFeature` is now the root reducer directly used in `DottonApp`
   - **`some ReducerOf<Self>` vs `some Reducer<State, Action>`** — The fix for the circular reference

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`Dotton/UI/Components/DotCircle.swift`** (created)
     - Defines `DotState` enum and `DotCircle` view for 5 circle states
     ```swift
     enum DotState: Equatable {
         case recorded(cell: PixelArtCell)
         case future(cell: PixelArtCell, dayNumber: Int)
         case missed(cell: PixelArtCell, dayNumber: Int)
         case preInstall(cell: PixelArtCell)
         case padding(cell: PixelArtCell)
     }
     struct DotCircle: View {
         let state: DotState
         var isToday: Bool = false
         private let size: CGFloat = 50
         // recorded → theme color, meetme font 24
         // future → EDECE9 bg, date number, gulim 18
         // missed → theme color 40% opacity + dashed inner stroke
         // today ring → F58583 strokeBorder 2.5pt
     }
     ```

   - **`Dotton/UI/Components/CalendarGrid.swift`** (created)
     - 7-column grid with weekday header (일월화수목금토)
     - Parameters: `month`, `theme`, `recordedDates: [Date]`, `installDate`, `today`, `onDayTap`
     - State logic: preInstall if before installDate, future if after today, today+unrecorded → future state with today ring, recorded/missed based on `recordedDates`
     ```swift
     struct CalendarGrid: View {
         let month: Date
         let theme: MonthlyTheme?
         let recordedDates: [Date]
         let installDate: Date
         let today: Date
         let onDayTap: (Int) -> Void
         private let columns = Array(repeating: GridItem(.fixed(50), spacing: 0), count: 7)
     }
     ```

   - **`Dotton/Features/Main/MainFeature.swift`** (created, multiple revisions)
     - Final version: `@Reducer` with `some Reducer<State, Action>` (NOT `ReducerOf<Self>`)
     - State uses `[Date]` not `[Diary]` to avoid @Model circular reference
     - `@Dependency(\.date)` removed (was potential issue), uses `Date()` directly
     ```swift
     @Reducer
     struct MainFeature {
         @ObservableState
         struct State: Equatable {
             var today: Date = Date()
             var currentMonth: Date = Calendar.current.startOfDay(for: Date()).startOfMonth
             var recordedDates: [Date] = []
             var installDate: Date = Date()
         }
         enum Action {
             case onAppear, prevMonthTapped, nextMonthTapped
             case dayTapped(Date), diariesLoaded([Date])
             case statisticsTapped, exportTapped
         }
         @Dependency(\.diaryClient) var diaryClient
         var body: some Reducer<State, Action> {
             Reduce<State, Action> { state, action in ... }
         }
     }
     extension MainFeature.State {
         init(today: Date, currentMonth: Date? = nil, installDate: Date) { ... }
     }
     ```

   - **`Dotton/Features/Main/MainView.swift`** (created, multiple revisions)
     - Full layout: FAF8F5 background, month header (year + nav), CalendarGrid, top-right buttons
     - Preview with mock data: testToday=2027/3/30, testInstall=2026/4/15, ~80% coverage
     - Pending 5 UI fixes (current work at summary time)
     ```swift
     struct MainView: View {
         @Bindable var store: StoreOf<MainFeature>
         // monthHeader: VStack(alignment: .leading) with year text + HStack(arrows + month)
         // TopRoundButtonStyle: 38×38 white background with Circle() (needs RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:13))
     }
     ```

   - **`Dotton/Features/AppFeature/AppFeature.swift`** (gutted to comments)
     ```swift
     // AppFeature는 여러 Feature를 조율할 필요가 생길 때 추가 예정
     // 현재는 MainFeature를 루트로 직접 사용
     ```

   - **`Dotton/Features/AppFeature/AppView.swift`** (gutted to comments)
     ```swift
     // AppView는 AppFeature 도입 시 추가 예정
     ```

   - **`Dotton/DottonApp.swift`** (updated)
     - Reads installDate from UserDefaults; creates `StoreOf<MainFeature>` directly
     ```swift
     @main
     struct DottonApp: App {
         let store: StoreOf<MainFeature> = {
             let installDate = UserDefaults... ?? Date()
             return Store(initialState: MainFeature.State(today: Date(), installDate: installDate)) {
                 MainFeature()
             }
         }()
         var body: some Scene {
             WindowGroup { MainView(store: store) }
             .modelContainer(for: Diary.self)
         }
     }
     ```

   - **`Info.plist`** (at project root, updated)
     - Added missing `CFBundleExecutable` key: `$(EXECUTABLE_NAME)`

   - **`Dotton/Core/Extensions/Date+Helpers.swift`** (updated previously)
     - Sunday-start `firstWeekdayOffset`: `return weekday - 1`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`@Reducer` circular reference (main error)**: "In expansion of macro 'Reducer' on struct 'MainFeature/AppFeature'"
     - Root cause: Xcode 26 / Swift 6.2 `Default Actor Isolation = MainActor` causes `some ReducerOf<Self>` to create a type resolution cycle during `@Reducer` macro expansion
     - Fix: Change `var body: some ReducerOf<Self>` → `var body: some Reducer<State, Action>`
     - Also removed `AppFeature` (was an extra `@Reducer` wrapping `MainFeature`), gutted `AppView`
     - TCA updated from 1.17.0 → 1.25.2 (ggaebiz reference uses 1.23.1 with swift-syntax 602.0.0)
   - **`[Diary]` in `@ObservableState` State**: SwiftData `@Model` class inside TCA `@ObservableState` causes macro expansion conflicts
     - Fix: Replace `var diaries: [Diary]` with `var recordedDates: [Date]`; map in `loadDiaries` effect
   - **Custom `init` in `@ObservableState` struct body**: Causes circular reference with macro-generated code
     - Fix: Move custom init to `extension MainFeature.State { init(...) }`
   - **CFBundleExecutable missing**: Simulator install failed
     - Fix: Added `<key>CFBundleExecutable</key><string>$(EXECUTABLE_NAME)</string>` to Info.plist
   - **Year showing "2,026"**: SwiftUI `Text("\(year)")` with Int uses locale-based formatting
     - Fix (pending): Use `Text(String(year))` or `Text(verbatim: ...)`
